intents.text: Why did this error occur after switching from openai-codex/gpt to github-copilot/gpt backend?
intents.verbatim_excerpt: why would that error happen after the last message?

intents.text: Clarify it was the GitHub Copilot backend that caused the error, not the Codex backend.
intents.verbatim_excerpt: it wasn't codex backend it was github copilot backend that fucked up

intents.text: Is the given conversation ID compatible with the Codex backend?
intents.verbatim_excerpt: is compatible with codex backend, no?

intents.text: Write an ad-hoc Node script using stream.test.ts patterns to reproduce the error with a synthetic context and complete call.
intents.verbatim_excerpt: do an ad-hoc node script see @packages/ai/test/stream.test.ts

intents.text: Could the error be caused by the ID having two underscores?
Try a string with the same underscore placement but different alphanumeric characters.
intents.verbatim_excerpt: try a string with the same _ placement but different alnum?

intents.text: Should we avoid converting '/' into '_' in the ID?
Only use a single underscore after 'fc' prefix in the ID format.
This underscore rule applies to 'foreign' IDs specifically.
What is the collision probability of the shortHash function?
intents.verbatim_excerpt: what's the collision probability between shortHash?

intents.text: Fix the ID generation and ensure the ID length stays within valid bounds.
intents.verbatim_excerpt: fix it, ensure lenght of id is within bounds!

intents.text: Test the fix first using the original problematic string from Copilot.
intents.verbatim_excerpt: test first with the string from copilot

intents.text: Remove all heap snapshot files in the current directory.
intents.verbatim_excerpt: remove all the heap snapshots in this dir

intents.text: Remove the mbtree file as well.
Update changelog, commit changed files, check branch is main, and push.
